issue.title: Desktop: Resolves #14763: add settings search to config screen
issue.body:
Fixes #14763 

## Problem

Desktop settings search did not work well as a navigation tool. Results were not clearly navigable from the sidebar, users could not jump directly to a specific setting row, and long labels could create sidebar layout pressure.

## Solution

This PR makes settings search sidebar-first:

- Show grouped matches under each section in the left sidebar (matching by section title, setting label, and description).
- Keep the right pane stable while typing (no auto-switch during search input).
- Switch to the detailed setting only when a sidebar search result is clicked.
- Allow long labels to wrap in the sidebar and show a no-results state when nothing matches.
- Add a clear button to quickly reset the search query.

## Test Plan

**Manual**

1. Open the desktop configuration screen.
2. Enter a query and confirm grouped matches appear under their original sections in the sidebar.
3. Confirm typing does not auto-switch the right pane.
4. Click a sidebar match and confirm section switch to the target setting.
5. Enter a query with no matches and confirm No results is shown.
6. Clear the query and confirm normal sidebar navigation returns.
7. Verify long labels wrap without widening the sidebar.

**Automated**

Added `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/Sidebar.test.tsx`. Covers:
- grouped sidebar search results under their section
- no-results state
- click event payload including `section` and `settingKey`

## Video
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63f53859-177f-4ba3-9c96-43dc6c7e5a2f

## AI Assistance Disclosure
- AI was used to help draft and refine this PR description (wording, structure, and clarity).
- AI was also used to suggest test-plan phrasing and summarize implementation intent.
- I have reviewed all AI-assisted content, verified it against the actual code changes, and I fully understand the submitted code and text.

---
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds a client-side search UI to the Config screen: new search state fields, a SearchInput in ConfigScreen, search/filtering and result UI in Sidebar (including keyboard navigation and settingKey selection), focus/scroll helpers for settings, new unit tests, and updated ignore/git rules for the test file. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Build & Ignore**
`/.eslintignore`, `/.gitignore`|Add ignore entries to exclude the new Sidebar test file `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/Sidebar.test.js`.| |**Shared config state**
`packages/lib/components/shared/config/config-shared.ts`|Add `searchQuery: string` and `searching: boolean` to `ConfigScreenState` and initialise them in `defaultScreenState`.| |**ConfigScreen**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/ConfigScreen.tsx`|Integrate `SearchInput` and pass `searchQuery`/`topContent` to `Sidebar`; add search state handlers; wrap each setting row with identifiable `id` attributes; add focus/scroll helpers to target settings by key.| |**Sidebar**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/Sidebar.tsx`|Add `topContent` and `searchQuery` props; compute grouped search results and `sectionsForNav`; render search results and no-results state; update keyboard navigation and selection to include optional `settingKey`; capture fixed sidebar width; export new styled components.| |**Tests**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/Sidebar.test.js`, `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/Sidebar.test.tsx`|Add tests verifying search filtering, no-results UI, and that selecting a search result emits `onSelectionChange` with the correct `section` and `settingKey`.| |**Integration tests & other**
`packages/app-desktop/integration-tests/models/SettingsScreen.ts`, `packages/app-desktop/integration-tests/settings.spec.ts`, `packages/lib/services/ReportService.test.ts`|Adjust integration locators and accessibility assertions to use sidebar anchors/`aria-current`; extend one test timeout.| ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram actor User participant SearchInput as "SearchInput (UI)" participant ConfigScreen as "ConfigScreen (Container)" participant Sidebar as "Sidebar (Nav)" participant SettingsList as "DOM / Settings List" User->>SearchInput: Type query SearchInput->>ConfigScreen: onSearchInputChange(query) ConfigScreen->>ConfigScreen: set searchQuery / set searching ConfigScreen->>Sidebar: pass searchQuery, topContent Sidebar->>Sidebar: compute searchResultGroups & sectionsForNav Sidebar->>SettingsList: render filtered sections & result items SettingsList->>User: display matching settings User->>SettingsList: click result item SettingsList->>Sidebar: trigger onClick (section, settingKey) Sidebar->>ConfigScreen: onSelectionChange(section, settingKey) ConfigScreen->>ConfigScreen: focusSettingByKey -> scroll/focus element ``` ## Suggested labels `desktop`, `search`, `accessibility` ## Suggested reviewers - mrjo118

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues

comments.body: ****CLA Assistant Lite bot**** All contributors have signed the CLA  ✍️ ✅

performed_via_github_app.name: GitHub Actions
performed_via_github_app.description: Automate your workflow from idea to production

comments.body: I have read the CLA Document and I hereby sign the CLA

Hello, do not quote the bot when putting your signature, simply put the signature in its own comment.

Also please don't forget to review the rabbit's comments

Hi laurent22, thanks for the clarification! 

I've signed the CLA in a separate comment. I'll also review the rabbit's comments and make the necessary updates.

I've made the following fixes:

- **Coderabbitai comment 1**: For setting search hits, I now detect `advanced` settings, expand Advanced first when needed, then scroll/focus in the state callback.
- **Coderabbitai comment 2**: I kept the inline visual layout, but fixed accessibility by switching the sidebar semantics to navigation (no tablist role-mixing) and ensuring one visible section item remains keyboard-focusable (`tabIndex={0}` fallback when selection is filtered out).
- **CI follow-up**: Updated settings integration test selectors/assertions to match the new sidebar semantics, and added a `20000ms` timeout to the slow `ReportService` decryption test to reduce flaky failures.
If you have any suggestions for further improvements or adjustments, I’d be glad to keep improving the Settings search experience!

How well does this work with a screen reader (see [Joplin: Testing with a screen reader](https://joplinapp.org/help/dev/accessibility#testing-with-a-screen-reader))?

**Note**: Previously, the settings screen followed the [tabpanel pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tabs/) to expose the settings tabs and content to screen readers and other accessibility tools. Some of the existing search components (e.g. go-to-anything) currently follow the [combobox pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/).

Hello, for information, the issue has been updated with detailed spec on how it should work:

https://github.com/laurent22/joplin/issues/14763

If you would like to continue working on this pull request, please check the spec.

Hi laurent22, thanks for the heads-up! I’ll take a look at the updated spec and see what needs to be adjusted in this PR.

Hi personalizedrefrigerator, thanks for pointing this out and for providing the full context and references, I really appreciate it.
I’ll verify how the new search behaves with a screen reader and review whether the current interaction still makes sense from an accessibility perspective, especially since parts of the search flow may now be closer to a combobox-style pattern.

Thanks for the updated spec and the clarification.

Since the new spec changes the behavior quite a bit, I reworked the feature in a new PR based on that updated direction: #14820.

I think that will make the review much clearer than continuing to evolve this older PR, so this one can be closed.
